- DOM stands for Document Object Model is an interface (API) for HTML and XML documents.

- When the browser first reads (parses) our HTML document it creates a big object, a really big object based on the HTML document this is the DOM. 

- It is a tree-like structure that is modeled from the HTML document. The DOM is used for interacting and modifying the DOM structure or specific Elements or Nodes.

Imagine if we have an HTML structure like this.
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

<head>
   <meta charset="UTF-8">
   <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
   <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="ie=edge">
   <title>Document Object Model</title>
</head>

<body>
   <div>
      <p>
         <span></span>
      </p>
      <label></label>
      <input>
   </div>
</body>

</html>

```
The DOM equivalent would be like this.

![DOM Equivalent](image.png)
The document object in JavaScript represents the DOM. It provides us many methods that we can use to selecting elements to update element contents and many more.


# DOM vs Virtual DOM vs Shadow DOM

This is one of the most common React interview questions.

From internal React learning materials:

* **Virtual DOM** is an in-memory representation of the Real DOM used by React for reconciliation. [\[What is ReactJS | Word\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/harsha_bobade_persistent_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B50675524-9F63-4DB9-89F4-9CE8E1744842%7D\&file=What%20is%20ReactJS.docx\&action=default\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1)
* **Shadow DOM** is a browser technology used for encapsulation and style scoping in Web Components. [\[What is ReactJS | Word\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/harsha_bobade_persistent_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B50675524-9F63-4DB9-89F4-9CE8E1744842%7D\&file=What%20is%20ReactJS.docx\&action=default\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1)

***

# 1. DOM (Real DOM)

DOM stands for:

```text
Document Object Model
```

It is the browser's representation of your HTML page.

## Example

&lt;body&gt;&nbsp; &lt;div&gt;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; &lt;h1&gt;Hello&lt;/h1&gt;&nbsp; &lt;/div&gt;&lt;/body&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Browser creates:

```text
Document
   │
   └── Body
          │
          └── Div
                 │
                 └── H1
```

***

## Updating Real DOM

```javascript
document
  .getElementById("title")
  .textContent =
  "React";
```

Browser performs:

```text
DOM Change
     ↓
Layout Calculation
     ↓
Paint
     ↓
Repaint
```

This can become expensive for large applications.

***

# 2. Virtual DOM (React)

Virtual DOM is:

```text
Lightweight JavaScript Object
```

stored in memory.

React keeps a copy of:

```text
Current UI
```

and

```text
Updated UI
```

in memory. [\[What is ReactJS | Word\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/harsha_bobade_persistent_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B50675524-9F63-4DB9-89F4-9CE8E1744842%7D\&file=What%20is%20ReactJS.docx\&action=default\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1)

***

## Example

```jsx
function App() {
  return (
    <h1>Hello React</h1>
  );
}
```

React creates:

```javascript
{
  type: "h1",
  props: {
    children: "Hello React"
  }
}
```

***

# Virtual DOM Update Process

```text
State Change
      ↓
New Virtual DOM
      ↓
Diffing Algorithm
      ↓
Find Changes
      ↓
Update Real DOM
```

***

## Example

```jsx
const [count,
       setCount] =
  useState(0);
```

Before:

&lt;h1&gt;0&lt;/h1&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

After:

&lt;h1&gt;1&lt;/h1&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

React updates only:

0 → 1![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

instead of rebuilding everything.

***

# React Reconciliation

```text
Old Virtual DOM
       ↓
New Virtual DOM
       ↓
Compare (Diffing)
       ↓
Patch Real DOM
```

This process is called:

```text
Reconciliation
```

and is part of React's rendering behaviour. [\[What is ReactJS | Word\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/harsha_bobade_persistent_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B50675524-9F63-4DB9-89F4-9CE8E1744842%7D\&file=What%20is%20ReactJS.docx\&action=default\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1)

***

# 3. Shadow DOM

Shadow DOM is a browser feature used mainly by:

```text
Web Components
```

for encapsulation. [\[What is ReactJS | Word\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/harsha_bobade_persistent_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B50675524-9F63-4DB9-89F4-9CE8E1744842%7D\&file=What%20is%20ReactJS.docx\&action=default\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1)

***

## Problem

Without Shadow DOM:

```css
button {
  color: red;
}
```

affects every button.

***

## Shadow DOM Solution

&lt;my-button&gt;&nbsp; #shadow-root&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; &lt;button&gt;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Save&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; &lt;/button&gt;&lt;/my-button&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Styles stay inside:

```text
Shadow Root
```

and don't leak outside.

***

## Example

```javascript
const host =
  document.querySelector(
    "#app"
  );

const shadowRoot =
  host.attachShadow({
    mode: "open"
  });

shadowRoot.innerHTML = `
  <style>
    button {
      color: red;
    }
  </style>

  <button>
    Click
  </button>
`;
```

***

# Visual Comparison

## Real DOM

```text
Browser DOM Tree
```

```text
HTML
 ↓
DOM Nodes
```

***

## Virtual DOM

```text
JavaScript Object Tree
```

```text
React
 ↓
Virtual DOM
 ↓
Real DOM
```

***

## Shadow DOM

```text
Browser DOM
 ↓
Web Component
 ↓
Private DOM Tree
```

***

# Comparison Table

| Feature       | DOM               | Virtual DOM      | Shadow DOM          |
| ------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | ------------------- |
| Purpose       | Browser Structure | Optimise Updates | Encapsulation       |
| Managed By    | Browser           | React            | Browser             |
| Performance   | Slower Updates    | Faster Updates   | Not for Performance |
| Memory        | Real Nodes        | JS Objects       | Real Nodes          |
| CSS Isolation | ❌                 | ❌                | ✅                   |
| Used In       | All Web Apps      | React/Vue        | Web Components      |

***

# React Example

```jsx
function Counter() {

  const [
    count,
    setCount
  ] = useState(0);

  return (
    <button
      onClick={() =>
        setCount(
          count + 1
        )
      }
    >
      {count}
    </button>
  );
}
```

Flow:

```text
Click
   ↓
State Change
   ↓
Virtual DOM Updated
   ↓
Diffing
   ↓
Real DOM Updated
```

***

# What is Shallow Rendering?

You wrote **"SHallow DOM"**, which is often confused with **Shallow Rendering** in React testing.

Shallow Rendering renders only the component being tested and does **not** render child components. This is useful for unit tests focused on a single component. [\[What is ReactJS | Word\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/harsha_bobade_persistent_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B50675524-9F63-4DB9-89F4-9CE8E1744842%7D\&file=What%20is%20ReactJS.docx\&action=default\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1)

Example:

```jsx
const renderer =
  new ShallowRenderer();

renderer.render(
  <MyComponent />
);
```

[\[What is ReactJS | Word\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/harsha_bobade_persistent_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B50675524-9F63-4DB9-89F4-9CE8E1744842%7D\&file=What%20is%20ReactJS.docx\&action=default\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1)

***

# React Interview Answer

```text
DOM         → Browser's actual document tree.

Virtual DOM → In-memory representation used by React
             to efficiently update the UI.

Shadow DOM  → Browser feature used by Web Components
             for style and DOM encapsulation.

Shallow Rendering → React testing technique that renders
                    a component one level deep.
```

### Easy Way to Remember

```text
DOM         = Real UI
Virtual DOM = React's Copy
Shadow DOM  = Private UI
```


From the React material I found:

* **Virtual DOM** is an in-memory representation of the Real DOM that React keeps and synchronises with the actual browser DOM through a process called reconciliation.
* **Shadow DOM** is a browser feature used for encapsulation of DOM and CSS, primarily in Web Components.
* **Shallow Rendering** is a React testing technique that renders a component only one level deep for unit testing.

***

# 1. How Virtual DOM Improves React Performance

## Problem with Real DOM

Imagine a list:

&lt;ul&gt;&nbsp; &lt;li&gt;React&lt;/li&gt;&nbsp; &lt;li&gt;Angular&lt;/li&gt;&nbsp; &lt;li&gt;Vue&lt;/li&gt;&lt;/ul&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Now you update only:

&lt;li&gt;Angular → Angular 20&lt;/li&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### Without React

Browser may perform:

```text
DOM Update
   ↓
Layout Calculation
   ↓
Repaint
   ↓
Reflow
```

DOM operations are comparatively expensive.

***

## Virtual DOM Approach

React creates a lightweight JavaScript representation.

### Old Virtual DOM

```javascript
{
  type: "ul",
  children: [
    "React",
    "Angular",
    "Vue"
  ]
}
```

### New Virtual DOM

```javascript
{
  type: "ul",
  children: [
    "React",
    "Angular 20",
    "Vue"
  ]
}
```

***

## Diffing Process

```text
Old Virtual DOM
      ↓
New Virtual DOM
      ↓
Compare
      ↓
Find Difference
      ↓
Update Only Changed Node
```

This process is called:

```text
Reconciliation
```

React updates only:

Angular → Angular 20![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

instead of rebuilding the whole UI.

***

## React Example

```jsx
function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] =
    useState(0);

  return (
    <>
      <h1>{count}</h1>

      <button
        onClick={() =>
          setCount(count + 1)
        }
      >
        Increment
      </button>
    </>
  );
}
```

Flow:

```text
Click
   ↓
State Change
   ↓
New Virtual DOM
   ↓
Diffing
   ↓
Update Only h1 Text
```

***

# 2. Shadow DOM Example with Web Components

Shadow DOM provides:

```text
DOM Isolation
CSS Isolation
Encapsulation
```

***

## Custom Web Component

```javascript
class UserCard
  extends HTMLElement {

  constructor() {
    super();

    const shadow =
      this.attachShadow({
        mode: "open"
      });

    shadow.innerHTML = `
      <style>
        .card {
          border: 1px solid black;
          padding: 10px;
        }

        button {
          background: blue;
          color: white;
        }
      </style>

      <div class="card">
        <h3>Sudhir</h3>
        <button>
          Save
        </button>
      </div>
    `;
  }
}

customElements.define(
  "user-card",
  UserCard
);
```

***

## Usage

&lt;user-card&gt;&lt;/user-card&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Browser creates:

```text
user-card
   ↓
#shadow-root
   ↓
card
   ↓
button
```

***

## Why Shadow DOM?

Without Shadow DOM:

```css
button {
  color: red;
}
```

affects every button.

With Shadow DOM:

```css
button {
  color: blue;
}
```

stays inside that component only.

***

# 3. Shallow Rendering vs Virtual DOM

Many developers confuse these.

***

## Virtual DOM

### Purpose

```text
Improve UI Rendering Performance
```

### Used By

```text
React Runtime
```

### How It Works

```text
State Change
      ↓
Virtual DOM
      ↓
Diffing
      ↓
Real DOM Update
```

Example:

```jsx
const [count,
       setCount] =
  useState(0);
```

React internally updates the Virtual DOM.

***

## Shallow Rendering

### Purpose

```text
Unit Testing
```

### Used By

```text
React Tests
```

### How It Works

Render only:

```text
Current Component
```

without rendering children.

***

### Example

```jsx
function UserCard() {
  return (
    <div>
      <Avatar />
      <UserInfo />
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Shallow Render

```jsx
const renderer =
  new ShallowRenderer();

renderer.render(
  <UserCard />
);
```

Only:

```jsx
<div>
  <Avatar />
  <UserInfo />
</div>
```

is tested.

The child components are not actually rendered.

***

# Comparison Table

| Feature            | Virtual DOM   | Shadow DOM             | Shallow Rendering |
| ------------------ | ------------- | ---------------------- | ----------------- |
| Purpose            | Performance   | Encapsulation          | Testing           |
| Used By            | React Runtime | Browser/Web Components | React Testing     |
| Updates UI         | ✅             | ✅                      | ❌                 |
| Isolates CSS       | ❌             | ✅                      | ❌                 |
| Uses Diffing       | ✅             | ❌                      | ❌                 |
| Used in Production | ✅             | ✅                      | ❌                 |
| Used in Unit Tests | ❌             | ❌                      | ✅                 |

***

# Senior React Interview Answer

```text
Real DOM:
  Browser's actual DOM tree.

Virtual DOM:
  React's in-memory copy of the DOM used
  for diffing and reconciliation to minimise
  expensive DOM updates.

Shadow DOM:
  Browser technology used by Web Components
  for DOM and CSS encapsulation.

Shallow Rendering:
  A React testing technique that renders only
  a component itself without rendering child
  components.
```

**Easy interview memory trick:**

```text
DOM         = Real UI
Virtual DOM = Fast UI Updates
Shadow DOM  = Private UI
Shallow Rendering = Component Testing
```


# Custom YouTube-Style Video Player in React

Features:

✅ Video playlist above player  
✅ Click thumbnail/title to load video  
✅ Play/Pause  
✅ Progress Bar  
✅ Current Time / Duration  
✅ Auto-play selected video  
✅ Accessible controls

***

## App.jsx

```jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import CustomVideoPlayer from "./CustomVideoPlayer";

const VIDEOS = [
  {
    id: 1,
    title: "React Tutorial",
    thumbnail:
      "https://picsum.photos/300/150?1",
    src:
      "https://www.w3schools.com/html/mov_bbb.mp4"
  },
  {
    id: 2,
    title: "JavaScript Tutorial",
    thumbnail:
      "https://picsum.photos/300/150?2",
    src:
      "https://www.w3schools.com/html/movie.mp4"
  },
  {
    id: 3,
    title: "TypeScript Tutorial",
    thumbnail:
      "https://picsum.photos/300/150?3",
    src:
      "https://www.w3schools.com/html/mov_bbb.mp4"
  }
];

export default function App() {
  const [selectedVideo, setSelectedVideo] =
    useState(VIDEOS[0]);

  return (
    <div
      style={{
        maxWidth: "900px",
        margin: "0 auto"
      }}
    >
      <h1>
        Custom Video Player
      </h1>

      {/* Playlist */}

      <div
        style={{
          display: "flex",
          gap: "20px",
          marginBottom: "20px"
        }}
      >
        {VIDEOS.map(video => (
          <div
            key={video.id}
            onClick={() =>
              setSelectedVideo(video)
            }
            style={{
              cursor: "pointer",
              width: "200px",
              border:
                selectedVideo.id ===
                video.id
                  ? "2px solid blue"
                  : "1px solid #ddd",
              padding: "10px"
            }}
          >
            {

            <p>{video.title}</p>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>

      {/* Video Player */}

      <CustomVideoPlayer
        video={selectedVideo}
      />
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# CustomVideoPlayer.jsx

```jsx
import {
  useRef,
  useState,
  useEffect
} from "react";

export default function CustomVideoPlayer({
  video
}) {
  const videoRef =
    useRef(null);

  const [playing,
         setPlaying] =
    useState(false);

  const [progress,
         setProgress] =
    useState(0);

  const [duration,
         setDuration] =
    useState(0);

  const [currentTime,
         setCurrentTime] =
    useState(0);

  // Auto load new video
  useEffect(() => {
    if (videoRef.current) {
      videoRef.current.load();
      videoRef.current.play();
      setPlaying(true);
    }
  }, [video]);

  const togglePlay =
    () => {
      const player =
        videoRef.current;

      if (!player) return;

      if (player.paused) {
        player.play();
        setPlaying(true);
      } else {
        player.pause();
        setPlaying(false);
      }
    };

  const handleTimeUpdate =
    () => {
      const player =
        videoRef.current;

      const percent =
        (player.currentTime /
          player.duration) *
        100;

      setProgress(percent);
      setCurrentTime(
        player.currentTime
      );
    };

  const handleLoadedMetadata =
    () => {
      setDuration(
        videoRef.current
          .duration
      );
    };

  const handleSeek = e => {
    const player =
      videoRef.current;

    const seekTime =
      (e.target.value /
        100) *
      player.duration;

    player.currentTime =
      seekTime;

    setProgress(
      e.target.value
    );
  };

  const formatTime =
    seconds => {
      const mins =
        Math.floor(
          seconds / 60
        );

      const secs =
        Math.floor(
          seconds % 60
        );

      return `${mins}:${String(
        secs
      ).padStart(2, "0")}`;
    };

  return (
    <div>
      <h2>{video.title}</h2>

      <video
        ref={videoRef}
        width="100%"
        onTimeUpdate={
          handleTimeUpdate
        }
        onLoadedMetadata={
          handleLoadedMetadata
        }
      >
        {video.src}
      </video>

      <div
        style={{
          marginTop: "10px"
        }}
      >
        <button
          onClick={togglePlay}
        >
          {playing
            ? "Pause"
            : "Play"}
        </button>

        <span
          style={{
            marginLeft: "15px"
          }}
        >
          {formatTime(
            currentTime
          )}
          {" / "}
          {formatTime(duration)}
        </span>
      </div>

      <input
        type="range"
        min="0"
        max="100"
        value={progress}
        onChange={handleSeek}
        style={{
          width: "100%",
          marginTop: "10px"
        }}
      />
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# Advanced Features (Senior React Interview Version)

You can further add:

```text
✅ Volume Control
✅ Mute / Unmute
✅ Fullscreen API
✅ Picture-in-Picture
✅ Playback Speed (0.5x,1x,1.5x,2x)
✅ Keyboard Shortcuts
✅ Video Buffering Indicator
✅ Video Quality Selector
✅ Video Playlist
✅ Captions/Subtitles
✅ Accessibility (WCAG)
✅ Mini Player
✅ Resume Last Watched Position
```

### Keyboard Shortcuts

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  const handleKeyDown = e => {
    const video =
      videoRef.current;

    switch (e.code) {
      case "Space":
        togglePlay();
        break;

      case "ArrowRight":
        video.currentTime += 10;
        break;

      case "ArrowLeft":
        video.currentTime -= 10;
        break;

      default:
        break;
    }
  };

  window.addEventListener(
    "keydown",
    handleKeyDown
  );

  return () =>
    window.removeEventListener(
      "keydown",
      handleKeyDown
    );
}, []);
```

This is very similar to how YouTube implements playlist selection + player controls in React applications.


Since you've worked on **WCAG-compliant React applications** and accessibility-focused projects, custom video controls are a great interview topic because they combine React, keyboard navigation, ARIA, focus management, and media APIs. [\[sudhira.jedheresume | PDF\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/sudhir_jedhe_persistent_com/Documents/Microsoft%20Copilot%20Chat%20Files/sudhira.jedheresume.pdf?web=1), [\[Sudhir A....e – Resume | PDF\]](https://persistentsystems-my.sharepoint.com/personal/sudhir_jedhe_persistent_com/Documents/Microsoft%20Teams%20Chat%20Files/Sudhir%20A.%20Jedhe%20%e2%80%93%20Resume.pdf?web=1)

# Why Custom Video Controls Need Accessibility

When you hide the browser's default controls:

&lt;video controls /&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

you lose built-in accessibility.

If you create:

```jsx
<button>▶</button>
```

you must manually implement:

✅ Keyboard support

✅ Screen reader support

✅ Focus management

✅ Announcements

✅ Captions

✅ ARIA attributes

***

# 1. Accessible Play / Pause Button

## ❌ Bad

```jsx
<div onClick={togglePlay}>
  Play
</div>
```

Problems:

```text
Not keyboard accessible
Not announced by screen readers
```

***

## ✅ Good

```jsx
<button
  onClick={togglePlay}
  aria-label={
    isPlaying
      ? "Pause video"
      : "Play video"
  }
>
  {isPlaying ? "⏸" : "▶"}
</button>
```

Screen Reader:

```text
Play Video Button
```

***

# 2. Keyboard Shortcuts

YouTube style.

```jsx
useEffect(() => {

  const handleKeyDown =
    e => {

      switch (e.code) {

        case "Space":
          togglePlay();
          break;

        case "ArrowRight":

          videoRef.current
            .currentTime += 10;

          break;

        case "ArrowLeft":

          videoRef.current
            .currentTime -= 10;

          break;

        case "KeyM":

          videoRef.current.muted =
            !videoRef.current
              .muted;

          break;
      }
    };

  window.addEventListener(
    "keydown",
    handleKeyDown
  );

  return () => {
    window.removeEventListener(
      "keydown",
      handleKeyDown
    );
  };

}, []);
```

### Accessible Keys

```text
Space → Play / Pause
← → Seek
M → Mute
F → Fullscreen
```

***

# 3. Accessible Progress Bar

## ❌ Bad

```jsx
<div className="progress" />
```

Screen readers know nothing about it.

***

## ✅ Good

```jsx
<input
  type="range"
  min="0"
  max="100"
  value={progress}
  onChange={handleSeek}
  aria-label="Video progress"
/>
```

Screen Reader:

```text
Video Progress Slider
50 Percent
```

***

# 4. Focus Management

One of the most important WCAG requirements.

## Auto Focus First Control

```jsx
const playButtonRef =
  useRef(null);

useEffect(() => {

  playButtonRef.current
    ?.focus();

}, []);
```

```jsx
<button ref={playButtonRef}>
  Play
</button>
```

***

## Focus After Loading Video

```jsx
const progressRef =
  useRef();

function handleVideoLoaded() {

  progressRef.current
    ?.focus();
}
```

***

# 5. Captions / Subtitles

Required for accessibility.

```jsx
<video controls>

  {videoUrl}

  captions.vtt

</video>
```

Screen readers and deaf users can access the content.

***

# 6. Live Region Announcements

Announce state changes.

```jsx
<div
  aria-live="polite"
  className="sr-only"
>
  {status}
</div>
```

***

## Example

```jsx
setStatus(
  "Video Paused"
);
```

Screen Reader announces:

```text
Video Paused
```

***

# 7. Volume Control

```jsx
<input
  type="range"
  min="0"
  max="1"
  step="0.1"
  value={volume}
  onChange={changeVolume}
  aria-label="Volume"
/>
```

***

# 8. Playlist Accessibility

For your YouTube-style playlist:

```jsx
<div
  role="listbox"
  aria-label="Video Playlist"
>
  {videos.map(video => (

    <button
      key={video.id}
      role="option"
      aria-selected={
        selectedVideo.id ===
        video.id
      }
    >
      {video.title}
    </button>

  ))}
</div>
```

Screen Reader:

```text
Video Playlist
React Tutorial Selected
```

***

# Focus Trap for Mini Player / Modal

If video opens inside a modal:

```jsx
<Tab>
```

should stay within modal controls.

Use:

```jsx
focus-trap-react
```

or custom focus trapping.

***

# WCAG Checklist for Custom Video Player

Based on accessibility best practices including keyboard navigation, ARIA usage, focus handling, and tab order guidance found in accessibility framework materials. [\[Addendum 6...framework | PowerPoint\]](https://persistentsystems.sharepoint.com/sites/EIS-SE-UserExperience/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B687A718E-18F6-4990-AA50-928DC8ED9989%7D\&file=Addendum%206_Comprehensive%20accessibility%20framework.pptx\&action=edit\&mobileredirect=true\&DefaultItemOpen=1), [\[AI\_Intevie...e\_00003252 | PDF\]](https://persistentsystems.sharepoint.com/sites/PersistentLearningandDevelopment/ResponsiveAssets/SLF/SLF%20AI-interview%20reports/HLS-SLF%20AI-interview%20reports/Myriad/AI_Inteview_Evaluation_Mahesh%20Ganjare_00003252.pdf?web=1)

```text
✅ Keyboard Operable

✅ Play/Pause Button

✅ Mute Button

✅ Volume Slider

✅ Seek Slider

✅ Captions

✅ Visible Focus Ring

✅ ARIA Labels

✅ Screen Reader Announcements

✅ Focus Management

✅ Accessible Playlist

✅ Fullscreen Support

✅ No Keyboard Trap
```

# Senior React Interview Answer

> When building custom video controls in React, accessibility must be implemented manually because native video accessibility is lost when browser controls are replaced. Essential features include keyboard-operable controls, ARIA labels, focus management with `useRef`, accessible sliders for seek and volume, caption tracks, live region announcements, and proper focus visibility. For a YouTube-style player, playlist items should use `role="option"` within a `role="listbox"` container, while video controls should remain fully operable via keyboard and screen readers to meet WCAG requirements.


# Shadow DOM Video Player (Web Component + React)

A **Shadow DOM Video Player** is a video player whose HTML, CSS, and controls are encapsulated inside a Shadow Root.

Benefits:

✅ CSS Isolation

✅ No style leakage

✅ Reusable Component

✅ Works across frameworks

✅ Better encapsulation

The React learning material notes that **Shadow DOM is a browser technology used for scoping DOM and CSS in Web Components**.

***

# Architecture

```text
React App
    ↓
<video-player>
    ↓
#shadow-root
    ↓
Video
Controls
Styles
```

Outside styles cannot affect the player.

***

# Custom Web Component

## video-player.js

```javascript
class VideoPlayer
  extends HTMLElement {

  constructor() {
    super();

    const shadow =
      this.attachShadow({
        mode: "open"
      });

    shadow.innerHTML = `
      <style>

        .player {
          width: 700px;
          background: #111;
          padding: 10px;
          border-radius: 8px;
        }

        video {
          width: 100%;
        }

        button {
          background: #ff0000;
          color: white;
          border: none;
          padding: 8px 16px;
          cursor: pointer;
        }

      </style>

      <div class="player">

        <video>
          
        </video>

        <div>

          <button id="playBtn">
            Play
          </button>

        </div>

      </div>
    `;
  }

  connectedCallback() {

    const video =
      this.shadowRoot.querySelector(
        "video"
      );

    const source =
      this.shadowRoot.querySelector(
        "source"
      );

    const playBtn =
      this.shadowRoot.querySelector(
        "#playBtn"
      );

    source.src =
      this.getAttribute(
        "src"
      );

    video.load();

    playBtn.addEventListener(
      "click",
      () => {

        if (
          video.paused
        ) {

          video.play();

          playBtn.textContent =
            "Pause";

        } else {

          video.pause();

          playBtn.textContent =
            "Play";
        }
      }
    );
  }
}

customElements.define(
  "video-player",
  VideoPlayer
);
```

***

# Using It In HTML

movie.mp4&lt;/video-player&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

The browser creates:

```text
video-player
      ↓
shadow-root
      ↓
video
button
styles
```

***

# Using Shadow DOM Component Inside React

## Register Component

```jsx
import "./video-player";
```

***

## React Component

```jsx
function App() {

  return (

    <div>

      <h1>
        Shadow DOM Video
      </h1>

      https://www.w3schools.com/html/mov_bbb.mp4

    </div>

  );
}
```

***

# YouTube-Style Playlist + Shadow Video Player

```jsx
const videos = [
  {
    title: "React",
    src: "/videos/react.mp4"
  },
  {
    title: "JavaScript",
    src: "/videos/js.mp4"
  }
];

function App() {

  const [currentVideo,
         setCurrentVideo] =
    useState(videos[0]);

  return (

    <>
      <div>

        {videos.map(video => (

          <button
            key={video.src}
            onClick={() =>
              setCurrentVideo(
                video
              )
            }
          >
            {video.title}
          </button>

        ))}

      </div>

      <video-player
        src={
          currentVideo.src
        }
      />

    </>

  );
}
```

***

# Accessibility Version

Inside Shadow DOM:

&lt;button&nbsp; aria-label="Play Video"&gt;&nbsp; Play&lt;/button&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

&lt;input&nbsp; type="range"&nbsp; aria-label="Seek Video"/&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

&lt;video&gt;&nbsp; captions.vtt&lt;/video&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Recommended support:

```text
Space      → Play/Pause
Arrow Left → Rewind
Arrow Right→ Forward
M          → Mute
F          → Fullscreen
```

***

# Shadow DOM vs React Component

## React Component

```jsx
function VideoPlayer() {
  return <video />;
}
```

```text
Styles can leak in/out
Lives in React Tree
```

***

## Shadow DOM Component

&lt;video-player&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

```text
Styles encapsulated
DOM encapsulated
Framework agnostic
```

***

# Senior React Interview Answer

> Shadow DOM is a browser feature used to encapsulate DOM and CSS within a Web Component. A Shadow DOM video player keeps its controls, styles, and markup isolated from the rest of the application, preventing style conflicts. In React, a Shadow DOM video player can be implemented as a custom Web Component and consumed like a normal HTML element. This approach is particularly useful for design systems, media players, embeddable widgets, and reusable components that must behave consistently across applications. The Shadow DOM provides encapsulation, whereas React's Virtual DOM focuses on efficient UI updates.
